_about: Seed playbook for lure — proven ad-hook winners, known losers, and house rules per channel. Researched 2026. Edit freely or grow your own with lure score / add-winner / add-loser.

winners.pattern: Curiosity gap — hint at a payoff without revealing it, so the reader has to open to close the loop
winners.example: We found something odd in your last campaign

winners.pattern: Specific number — lead with a digit; specificity reads as proof and lifts opens
winners.example: 7 subject lines that doubled our open rate

winners.pattern: Direct benefit — name the exact outcome and remove the guesswork
winners.example: How to double your list in 30 days

winners.pattern: Question that mirrors their internal monologue
winners.example: Are you still paying full price for this?

winners.pattern: Behavioral personalization — reference what they actually did, not a [first name] token
winners.example: About the pricing page you looked at

winners.pattern: Real deadline — true scarcity, never invented
winners.example: Last call, doors close tonight at midnight

winners.pattern: Body open: relatable pain then you are not alone then pivot to the fix
winners.example: Ever feel like your to-do list runs your life? You are not alone. Here is the one tweak that fixed it for us.

winners.pattern: Body open: pain-point-first for cold B2B so the reader self-qualifies in one line
winners.example: Most agencies lose a third of their leads to slow follow-up. Here is the fix.

losers.avoid: free, guaranteed, no obligation, 100% free
losers.why: Classic spam fingerprints that trip Bayesian filters and hurt deliverability, especially clustered with other trigger words

losers.avoid: ALL CAPS and multiple exclamation marks (!!!, $$$)
losers.why: Formatting tells that mark a sender as spam and lower opens even when the offer is legit

losers.avoid: This is not spam, not a scam, no strings attached
losers.why: Saying you are not spam is one of the strongest spam signals that exists; legitimate senders never need it

losers.avoid: [first name]-only personalization
losers.why: Overused, decaying signal that advertises a mass template rather than real relevance

losers.avoid: Quick question, Just following up, Re: (fake reply thread)
losers.why: Data-backed lowest performers; they bait the open, the reader feels tricked, and reply quality collapses

losers.avoid: I hope this email finds you well, I wanted to reach out, My name is X and I work at Y
losers.why: Email white noise that signals zero research and gets deleted by humans, tanking reply rate

losers.avoid: Synergy, leverage, circle back, move the needle, pick your brain
losers.why: Corporate buzzwords read as automated and human-less, which kills replies

losers.avoid: Subject lines over ~50 characters
losers.why: Truncate on mobile where most opens happen, so the hook never lands

houseRules: Keep subject lines under 50 characters; 2 to 4 word subjects often outperform
Optimize the subject and preview text as a pair, never leave preview auto-generated (10 to 15% lift)
The first body line has about 3 seconds to earn the second; lead with pain or curiosity, not yourself
Every claimed deadline or scarcity must be real or it costs trust and deliverability
Behavior-based context beats token-merge personalization every time

winners.pattern: PAS — problem, agitate, then solve, so the relief feels urgent
winners.example: Worried you are behind on retirement? Watching the market swing makes it worse, and winging it feels like a gamble. Here is how to build a plan that lets you sleep at night.

winners.pattern: Callout that mirrors internal dialogue and filters for the right person
winners.example: Coaches, are you making this one pricing mistake?

winners.pattern: Specific contrarian or named mistake as a pattern interrupt
winners.example: Stop boosting posts. It is quietly burning your budget.

winners.pattern: Open loop with a specific result the reader has to resolve
winners.example: This should not have worked, but our cost per lead dropped 40%

winners.pattern: Benefit plus specificity headline inside the 40-char limit
winners.example: Cut ad costs 40% in 30 days

winners.pattern: Offer-forward headline that removes friction
winners.example: First month free, no card

losers.avoid: Are you broke / overweight / in debt / struggling with X?
losers.why: Meta auto-rejects ads that imply knowledge of a personal or protected attribute; the you-plus-negative framing is the #1 trigger

losers.avoid: Before and after images, lose 20 lbs in 2 weeks, get rid of your belly fat
losers.why: Banned for generating negative self-perception to sell health and weight-loss products

losers.avoid: Miracle, doctors hate this, one weird trick, shocking, you won't believe
losers.why: Sensational and unrealistic-outcome policy violations that also crush quality ranking even when not rejected

losers.avoid: Like if, tag a friend, comment YES, share to win
losers.why: Engagement bait that Meta demotes; CPMs balloon even without a rejection

losers.avoid: Generic vague hooks like Here is a business tip
losers.why: Loses every time to a specific hook; the wrong people scroll past and the right people never self-ID

losers.avoid: Burying the hook past character 125
losers.why: It gets truncated behind See more and is never read on the scroll

houseRules: Front-load the first 80 to 125 characters as if they are the whole ad; that is what shows before See more
The hook earns the next second, it does not sell; open a loop or trigger emotion within about 2 seconds
Signal who the ad is for in the first frame so the right reader self-IDs
Flip you-plus-a-negative into benefit framing (not Are you broke, but Financial tools for modern budgets)
Respect the specs: primary text 125, headline 40, description 30 characters
When generating variants, vary the hook mechanism (question vs contrarian vs number vs PAS), not just the wording

winners.pattern: USP or specificity headline with a number for instant credibility
winners.example: Trusted by 10,000+ teams

winners.pattern: Benefit or outcome headline naming the result the searcher wants
winners.example: Acne-free skin in 2 weeks

winners.pattern: Benefit-led CTA that beats the generic Learn More
winners.example: Get a free quote today

winners.pattern: Price or discount lever for price-conscious intent
winners.example: Free shipping on all orders

winners.pattern: Question headline that echoes the search query
winners.example: Tired of slow WiFi?

winners.pattern: Social proof headline with borrowed, scannable credibility
winners.example: Rated 4.9 by 2,000 users

winners.pattern: Keyword-match headline (use in 3 to 4 of 15 slots for relevance)
winners.example: Affordable CRM software

losers.avoid: Guaranteed results, proven to work, 100% effective, #1 rated
losers.why: Suggests improbable or unguaranteeable outcomes; auto-flagged under Unreliable Claims. Swap for may help, designed to, many users report

losers.avoid: Double your money, get rich quick, beat the market, guaranteed income
losers.why: Explicitly prohibited get-rich-quick language: large return with minimal risk or effort

losers.avoid: Keyword stuffing every one of the 15 headlines
losers.why: 2026 algorithms read semantics; stuffing now hurts Quality Score instead of helping

losers.avoid: Near-duplicate headlines
losers.why: Too-similar assets suppress the ad from serving at all

losers.avoid: Over-pinning headlines
losers.why: Restricts the ML from recombining assets; pin only for brand or legal reasons

losers.avoid: Learn More as the CTA for lead gen
losers.why: Weak and generic; benefit-led CTAs like Get a free quote convert measurably better

houseRules: Feed Google diverse, non-overlapping assets so its ML can recombine; mix keyword, benefit, CTA, and social-proof types
Fill 8 to 10 strong unique headlines rather than forcing 15 weak ones
Vary headline length so the system has short and medium options to test
Moving Ad Strength from Poor to Excellent averages about 15% more clicks and conversions
Every claim in the ad must be substantiated on the landing page or it risks disapproval

winners.pattern: Specific-use-case framing so the reader self-identifies the exact need
winners.example: Best running shoes for flat feet

winners.pattern: Pain-point to product path where the offer is the natural resolution
winners.example: How to fix slow WordPress load times

winners.pattern: Core angle scaled into mini-angles to multiply one validated winner
winners.example: Learn the skill you have avoided in 30 minutes a day, no prior knowledge needed, right from your phone

winners.pattern: Curiosity or contrarian loop that pays off fast
winners.example: The real reason your funnel keeps failing is not what you think

winners.pattern: Social proof through screenshots, testimonials, or switcher counts
winners.example: Over 8,000 people already made the switch

winners.pattern: Emotional or outcome angle segmented by the door it opens (seasonal, health, appearance)
winners.example: Train for longevity, not just for looks

winners.pattern: Real exclusivity or early access for genuine FOMO
winners.example: Early access before the public launch

losers.avoid: Make $10,000/month doing nothing, copy-paste your way to passive income, no skills needed
losers.why: Textbook get-rich-quick coding; trips platform deceptive-earnings policies and reads as a scam to savvy readers

losers.avoid: Secret method, hidden loophole, what the gurus do not want you to know
losers.why: Conspiracy-coded framing that networks treat as misleading and audiences read as manipulation

losers.avoid: Only 3 spots left and Doors close at midnight when repeated every week
losers.why: Repeated fake scarcity is the fastest trust-killer; once the reader catches the reset, every other claim is discounted

losers.avoid: World-class, revolutionary, game-changer, cutting-edge, industry-leading
losers.why: Unbelievable and unspecific; plain-English headlines have tested far higher than vague superlatives

losers.avoid: Generic Best X roundups with no use-case qualifier
losers.why: Convert far worse than a specific-use-case angle that lets the reader self-identify

losers.avoid: Curiosity hooks that intrigue but never pay off quickly
losers.why: An open loop with no fast explanation loses the reader before the click converts

houseRules: Refine a proven angle over chasing a brand-new one; creative quality drives about 3x more sales impact than frequency
Turn one winning angle into many mini-angles instead of constantly inventing new ones
Curiosity must pay off fast; the hook intrigues, the next line explains
Specific numbers and named segments beat superlatives every time
Any specific-result testimonial needs a results-vary disclaimer to stay compliant
